name: tomat built-in toolkit
description: Reference toolkit bundling the starter tools: web search and page reading, a calculator, date and time, downloads (fetch one or organize the folder), opening URLs in the browser, reading and writing the user's documents, scheduled prompts, saving structured rows to a private database, and a UI-walkthrough for the askUser flow. Useful as both a fresh-install starter and a worked example for third-party toolkit authors.

tools.name: download_url
tools.description: Download the file at a URL into the user's Downloads folder, reporting progress.

url.description: Absolute http(s) URL of the file to download.

filename.description: Optional filename for the saved file. Defaults to the last segment of the URL.

triggers: save that file to my downloads
grab the pdf from this link

read.reason: Check the Downloads folder for existing files and pick a non-clobbering name

env.reason: Honor the user's preferred Downloads folder (Linux/XDG)

tools.name: open_website
tools.description: Open a web URL in the user's default browser. Use when the user asks to visit, open, or go to a website.

url.description: Absolute http:// or https:// URL to open.

triggers: take me to the github page

run.reason: Hand the URL off to macOS's `open` command
Hand the URL off to Linux's `xdg-open`
Hand the URL off to Windows's default protocol handler

tools.name: askuser_demo
tools.description: Demonstrate the askUser flow: text input, single-select, multiselect with freeform input.

tools.name: write_document
tools.description: Create a document, or replace an existing document's content. Documents are markdown notes the user keeps in tomat; prefer edit_document for small changes to an existing document.

title.description: Document title. Creates the document if no document with this title exists.

content.description: Full markdown content for the document. Replaces any existing content.

triggers: save these notes as a document
create a new document called shopping list
replace the contents of my notes document

documents.reason: Create the document or replace its content

tools.name: edit_document
tools.description: Edit a document by replacing one exact text occurrence. The find text must appear exactly once; include enough surrounding context to make it unique.

title.description: Title of the document to edit.

find.description: Exact text to find. Must match exactly one place in the document.

replace.description: Replacement text. May be empty to delete the found text.

triggers: update the meeting time in my notes document
fix the typo in that document
change the deadline in my plan document
add milk to my shopping list document

documents.reason: Apply the find/replace edit to the document

tools.name: read_document
tools.description: Read the full content of one of the user's documents by title.

title.description: Title of the document to read.

triggers: what does my plan document say
look at the shopping list document

tools.name: schedule_prompt
tools.description: Propose a scheduled prompt: at the scheduled times, tomat opens an automated session that sends the instruction as a prompt. The user reviews the proposal in chat (and may edit it) before it is saved, so fill the fields with your best suggestion. Times are the user's local time.

title.description: Short name for the scheduled prompt, also used as the session title when it fires.

instruction.description: The prompt to send when the schedule fires. Write it as a request from the user, e.g. "Summarize the headlines from my feeds document."

kind.description: When the prompt repeats: once at a date-time, every N minutes, on weekdays at a time, monthly on a day, or yearly on a date.

at.description: For kind "once": local date-time formatted "YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM".

everyMinutes.description: For kind "interval": minutes between runs, 1 or more.

weekdays.description: For kind "weekly": weekdays to run on, 0 (Sunday) to 6 (Saturday).

month.description: For kind "yearly": month to run in, 1 to 12.

day.description: For kinds "monthly" and "yearly": day of the month, 1 to 31 (clamped to shorter months).

hour.description: For kinds "weekly", "monthly", and "yearly": hour to run at, 0 to 23.

minute.description: For kinds "weekly", "monthly", and "yearly": minute to run at, 0 to 59.

runMissed.description: When true, a run missed while tomat was off is made up once on the next start.

triggers: remind me every monday at 9am to fill in the timesheet
every morning give me a summary of my notes
schedule a prompt for tomorrow at 8
check the news once every hour

tools.name: show_document
tools.description: Show a document to the user, rendered as markdown in the chat. Use when the user wants to see a document rather than have it summarized.

title.description: Title of the document to show.

triggers: show me my meeting notes document
open my plan document in the chat

documents.reason: Read the document to display it

tools.name: get_datetime
tools.description: Get the current date and time in the user's local timezone, including the weekday and UTC offset. Use to anchor relative dates like "tomorrow" or "next friday" instead of guessing.

tools.name: calculator
tools.description: Evaluate a math expression and return the exact result. Supports arithmetic, comparisons, and functions like sqrt, sin, cos, log, min, max. Use for any calculation instead of doing the math yourself.

expression.description: The expression to evaluate, e.g. "(2 + 3) * sqrt(16)".

triggers: what is 2 to the power of 20

tools.name: fetch_webpage
tools.description: Fetch a web page and return its readable text content. Use to read an article, a docs page, or any URL the user mentions.

url.description: Absolute http(s) URL of the page to read.

triggers: open the link and tell me the gist

net.reason: Fetch the page the user asked to read

tools.name: web_search
tools.description: Search the web (DuckDuckGo) and return the top results as title, URL, and snippet. Use when the user asks about something current or outside your knowledge; follow up with fetch_webpage to read a result.

maxResults.description: How many results to return, 1 to 10.

triggers: search the web for deno 2 release notes
look up the weather in amsterdam
search for how tall the eiffel tower is

tools.name: organize_downloads
tools.description: Tidy the user's Downloads folder: the user picks which loose files to organize and reviews the proposed layout, then the accepted files are moved into category folders (Images, Documents, Archives, ...).

read.reason: List the files in the Downloads folder

env.reason: Honor the user's preferred Downloads folder (Linux/XDG)

tools.name: collect_table
tools.description: Save structured rows the user mentions (expenses, contacts, scores, ...) into the toolkit's private database. Propose columns and rows from the conversation; the user reviews and edits the table before anything is saved.

collection.description: Name of the collection to save into, e.g. "expenses".

rows.description: Proposed rows, one array of cell values per row, in column order.

triggers: save these expenses to a table
add this to my expenses collection
